section: Execution Order — SEQUENCED PLAN OF RECORD
verbatim: The dependency-ordered execution plan derived from this ledger is docs/PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md (S0–S14, with verified data-state receipts and the critique of prior sequencing).

One receipt inside it is already stale: line 71 'shelf_reserve as a symbol does not exist' is superseded by merge f049041 (registry_librarian shelf_reserve row) — the plan file itself needs Rule-3 restamping when next edited.

section: Execution Order — SEQUENCED PLAN OF RECORD
verbatim: Execute in S-order; a step's checklist anchors are listed per row. This ledger stays the item-level source of truth; the plan file is its ordering.
action: Enforce at every dispatch: before starting any step, re-read the step's checklist anchors from the ledger on disk and confirm the step's S-slot is the lowest unfinished S; record ledger-line anchors in the work item.
who: junior-agent (dispatch preamble), enforced by orchestration prompt
why: Ledger-as-item-truth plus plan-as-ordering prevents the C1 class of out-of-order paid work.
feasibility_note: Mechanically checkable: S0 done (327da45), S3 evidence gathered awaiting owner sign-off (336dc65) — current lowest open slots are S1/S2 (parallelizable with S3 per plan line 95), which matches open tasks #18/#20. Consistent with the repo CLAUDE.md no-cached-plan rule.
required_receipt: Each dispatched work item cites its S-slot + ledger line anchors read from disk at dispatch time.

verbatim: Capture-before-rebuild: no summary/lexicon/card rebuild may run while an ADOPTED capture hook targeting the same rows is unbuilt. A rebuild must list the open capture hooks for its seam and obtain an explicit owner waiver, else the hook lands first.
action: Before ANY summary/lexicon/card rebuild, run a pre-flight that lists ADOPTED-but-unbuilt capture hooks for the target rows (currently: T-HOOK-2 temporal_class/time_expressions — S1 unbuilt) and blocks unless an explicit owner waiver line is added to the ledger.
where: Ghost A summary contract seam (backend/services/ingestion/summary_semantics.py), corpus_lexicon rematerialization, services/librarian/card_builder.py; ledger Standing Rules block
why: Prevents re-buying paid passes (C1 cost: ~2,000 back-level paid v2 regenerations + a dedicated S4 pass).
feasibility_note: Concrete right now: S1 (T-HOOK-2) is unbuilt, so this rule currently BLOCKS any pair summary regen and the S8 card rebuild until S1/S4 land — exactly the plan's ordering. TERMINOLOGY: 'rebuild' spans three distinct seams (summary regen, lexicon rematerialization, card projection); the pre-flight must check hooks per-seam, not globally.
required_receipt: Every rebuild run log opens with 'open capture hooks for this seam: [...]' and either 'none' or an owner-waiver ledger line; violation count stays 1 (C1/C2).

verbatim: (Violated once — critique C1/C2; never again.)
action: Accept receipt — verified: PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md C1 (lines 99-101, rebuild-before-capture on P0.1 regen) and C2 (lines 102-103, 673 cards built with empty latent input) document the single violation.
why: The recorded violation is the evidentiary basis for the standing rule's adoption.
feasibility_note: Both critique entries read and match the claim: pair latent coverage 0, only 639/130,503 v2 parents carry latent_concepts, zero temporal_class anywhere (critique section 2, V).
required_receipt: C1/C2 text present in the plan file; 'never again' enforced via HDR:L16's pre-flight receipt.

verbatim: No dark fields: every captured field ships with its first consumer, or a named consumer + checklist anchor in the same phase; otherwise the ledger must mark it dark data.
action: Maintain a dark-data ledger check: for every captured field, grep for a reader; currently mark and track: (1) latent_concepts[].aliases — parsed/clamped, read by NOTHING (consumer lands S5); (2) latent_concepts dropped from Qdrant summary payloads (qdrant_writer.py:877-928 whitelist); (3) page_start — writers+readers exist, 0 rows populated on all 4 corpora (only a reingest lane can fill it, S3 matrix).
why: Dark fields silently invalidate every measurement taken while they accumulate (critique C3).
feasibility_note: The three current dark items are registry-confirmed (registry_latent collision note; registry_hierarchy page_start row 'implemented-but-dead'). S5 is the named consumer for aliases — per this rule S5 must CONSUME the existing captured aliases, not re-capture them.
required_receipt: Ledger contains an explicit 'dark data' marker for each consumer-less field, cleared only by a merged consumer with a checklist anchor (aliases cleared at S5).

verbatim: Receipts refresh tags: any status tag (IN PROGRESS/PARTIAL) contradicted by a later verification is stale by definition; ledger edits re-run the relevant verify and restamp the date.
action: Apply the rule NOW to three known-stale spots: (1) P0.1's two IN PROGRESS tags (verify PASSES all 4 corpora — critique line 19/70); (2) the Current Durable Baseline empty-summary_model lines L77-L84 (contradicted by the same green verify: 0 empty-model, 86,880+9,453+1,009+203 attributed); (3) L93's embedder /health sentence (contradicted by S0 receipt 327da45). Re-run the relevant verify, restamp dates.
why: Stale tags break the header's contract that a checkbox equals deployed + reconciled + verified.
feasibility_note: All three stale spots have fresh verifications on record (critique V receipts + S0 commit message with live :8082/health output). Cheap ledger edit + one script re-run.
required_receipt: p0_1_summary_integrity.py verify exit 0 pasted with date; ledger diff removing/restamping the three stale passages.

verbatim: One migration per seam: migrations touching the same artifact are co-scheduled (P2.5 + T-MAIN edges; S5 rollup + S7 facet cleanup payloads).
action: Enforce co-scheduling at plan level: P2.5 typed-signature edge migration ships in the same migration window as T-MAIN versioned RELATES_TO edges (S11/S12 pairing); S7 facet-cleanup payload rewrites ride S5's lexicon/payload rematerialization (payloads rewrite once). Pre-flight any migration by asking 'what else is scheduled to touch this artifact?'
who: junior-agent (migration planner) + owner sign-off on migration windows
where: Neo4j edges (RELATES_TO), Qdrant summary/parent payloads, corpus_lexicon collections
why: Double-touching durable artifacts doubles cost and doubles the chance of a half-migrated store.
feasibility_note: The two named pairings match the plan rows exactly (S5+S7 payload batch, S12 'co-scheduled with P2.5 edge migration (one migration)'). TERMINOLOGY: 'seam' here means durable artifact, not code seam — the rule binds data migrations, not code merges.
required_receipt: Each migration PR/run log names its seam and lists co-scheduled migrations; S5 and S7 payload rewrites appear as ONE Qdrant pass in run logs.

verbatim: Read-before-act: agents re-read the relevant section of THIS FILE from disk at every phase start and before dispatching work — never from session memory (see repo CLAUDE.md north-star section).
action: Enforce mechanically: every phase-start and dispatch message must quote the ledger section (with line numbers) as read from disk in that turn; forbid acting on remembered plan state.
who: junior-agent (all agents), per repo CLAUDE.md north-star section
why: Session-memory execution is how adopted hooks got skipped while their seam was hot (C1).
feasibility_note: Already codified in polymath CLAUDE.md (no-cached-plan-execution, commit 6f9becb per user memory). Verification is procedural: transcripts show a fresh Read of the ledger before each dispatch.
required_receipt: Dispatch transcript contains a same-turn disk read of the cited ledger lines.

verbatim: Scope: ingestion, hierarchy, retrieval, librarian behavior, concept vocabulary, GLiNER/RunPod parity, lifecycle hygiene, and all three retrieval tiers.
action: Re-check scope completeness against the ledger's section list whenever a new work family is added; no build step.
why: Declared scope bounds what the regression matrix and strict-ready definition must cover.
feasibility_note: The eight scope areas map 1:1 onto existing checklist sections (ingestion P0.x, hierarchy P0.2, retrieval P1.x, librarian phases, concept vocabulary P1.7/P2.1, GLiNER/RunPod parity P2.6/P2.7, lifecycle P0.6, three tiers = invariant 1 + regression matrix).
required_receipt: None beyond the ledger itself; scope change requires an owner-signed ledger edit.

verbatim: This file is the implementation ledger. A checkbox is complete only when the code is deployed, durable data is reconciled where required, and the listed acceptance tests pass. Code existing by itself is not completion.
action: Enforce the completion definition at every box flip: a [x] edit must cite (a) deploy receipt (image rebuild/restart or sidecar redeploy), (b) durable-data reconciliation where applicable, (c) acceptance test run with exit code.
why: 'Code existing is not completion' is the direct antidote to the dark-code failure (C3: 6 boxes sat IN CODE unmerged).
feasibility_note: S0's commit 327da45 is the model receipt (live :8082/health output + in-container suite counts + probe exit 0 in the commit message). Matches user's assert-before-commit and deploy-train rules (plan line 75).
required_receipt: Every [x] flip commit message contains deploy + reconcile + acceptance evidence, S0-style.

verbatim: `[x]` implemented, tested, deployed, and verified.
action: No build step; apply the definition when auditing: treat any [x] lacking a deploy/verify receipt as suspect and re-verify per Standing Rule 3.
why: Shared tag semantics keep multi-agent audits comparable.
feasibility_note: Definition is consistent with L34's completion contract; no conflict found in registries.
required_receipt: None — definitional.

verbatim: `[ ]` not complete.
action: No build step; auditors treat [ ] as open regardless of how much code exists (IN CODE annotations do not flip boxes).
why: Prevents dark code from masquerading as done.
feasibility_note: Consistent with C3's finding; the S0 pass demonstrated the correct flip path (IN CODE -> live-verified -> [x]).
required_receipt: None — definitional.

verbatim: Partial means some sub-items are complete but the parent outcome is not.
action: No build step; when reporting, count Partial parents as OPEN in rollups (as the plan critique's status table does).
why: Parent-outcome honesty stops sub-item progress from inflating completion.
feasibility_note: Live examples exist and are correctly tagged: P0.4 contradiction checks PARTIAL @f2fb6e2 (re-scoped in S0 commit, explicitly NOT built), P0.6 lease matrix PARTIAL.
required_receipt: None — definitional; rollup tables count Partial as open.

verbatim: Experiment means no production adoption until before/after evaluation.
action: No build step; gate any Experiment-tagged item (P3.4 quantization, P3.5 reranker serving) behind a recorded before/after evaluation prior to production adoption; clone-first where tagged.
why: Keeps experiments from leaking into production without an eval receipt.
feasibility_note: TERMINOLOGY (registry_eval 'before/after'): the before/after GATE is itself to-be-built — nearest seam is re-running run_heldout_eval per tier and diffing EVAL_*.json summaries; do not confuse with 'candidate counts before/after fusion' (per-query stage counts, checklist:1246).
required_receipt: Any experiment adoption cites paired EVAL_*.json (before/after) artifacts.

verbatim: Migration means existing durable artifacts must be changed or rebuilt.
action: No build step; when a Migration-tagged item runs, apply Standing Rule 4 (one migration per seam) and take pre-migration backups as done for P0.x (docs/baselines/p0_*_backups pattern).
why: Migration semantics distinguish durable-data work (needs reconciliation + backup) from code work.
feasibility_note: Backup convention already exercised 4 times (p0_1/p0_2/p0_5/p0_8 backup dirs exist in docs/baselines/). Consistent with the user's non-destructive snapshot+restore rule.
required_receipt: Every migration run has a pre-image backup path + post-migration reconcile count in its log.

verbatim: Every retrieval change is tested through Fast (`qdrant_only`), Hybrid (`qdrant_mongo`), and Graph (`qdrant_mongo_graph`).
action: Enforce per retrieval-change PR: run the three-tier probe (three_tier_eval / probe_tier_latency) across RetrievalTier.qdrant_only, qdrant_mongo, qdrant_mongo_graph and attach per-tier results; assert requested_tier == effective_tier in diagnostics to catch silent graph->hybrid collapse.
where: backend three_tier_eval.py, eval_metrics.py:214 (tier alias map), models/_schemas_legacy.py:1204-1205 (requested_tier/effective_tier); chat_orchestrator.py:3952-3953
why: A change validated on one tier routinely regresses another; tier collapse hides Graph regressions entirely.
feasibility_note: Harness exists (EVAL_2026-07-13_*.json captured per tier). TERMINOLOGY (registry_retrieval 'tier'): 'tier' is 4-way overloaded — RetrievalTier (this invariant), SourceChunk.source_tier, Tier-0 routing (NOT a RetrievalTier), and tier_chunker Tiers A/B/C. 'Fast' is the checklist alias for qdrant_only (eval_metrics.py:214 is authoritative).
required_receipt: Per-change: three EVAL/probe artifacts (one per tier) with requested_tier==effective_tier asserted; graph tier not silently collapsed.

verbatim: Cross-corpus tests preserve the exact user-selected corpus boundary.
action: Enforce via the cross-corpus heldout rows + an assertion that every returned evidence row's corpus_id is a member of the user-selected corpus_ids set (no leakage in, no silent drop of a selected corpus).
where: retriever corpus_ids filtering; heldout suite cross-corpus/cross-corpus-irrelevant shapes; reservation_policy corpus_floor diagnostics
why: Corpus boundary is the user's explicit trust boundary; leakage is a correctness and privacy failure.
feasibility_note: P0.3 is 10/11 done with only the acceptance row open ('cross-corpus retains evidence from each necessary corpus' — critique table row P0.3), so the machinery exists; the standing gate is the eval assertion. Note 'shelf' in P0.3's corpus_floor.skipped diagnostics means CORPUS (registry_librarian 'shelf' collision).
required_receipt: Cross-corpus eval rows pass with per-row corpus_id membership assertion; corpus_floor.skipped reports reasons for any omitted selected corpus.

verbatim: Retrieval remains top-down: corpus/shelf -> document -> hierarchy -> parent/child evidence.
action: Enforce order-of-operations in code review + trace audit: Tier-0 document routing precedes tier retrieval; descent uses document_gated_adaptive_tree_descent; no lane may fetch child evidence for documents never seated/routed.
where: Tier-0 routing (polymath_doc_summaries projection), summary_tree descent (document_gated_adaptive_tree_descent), retriever lanes
why: Top-down is the owner architecture's core shape; bottom-up leakage reintroduces the flat-chunk soup RAPTOR work exists to kill.
feasibility_note: TERMINOLOGY, two collisions: (a) 'corpus/shelf' — 'shelf' means CORPUS here (P1.4 sense), not the P1.5 shelf-role bucket (registry_librarian); (b) 'hierarchy' descent from shelf_reserve seats is NOT yet wired — descent-from-Tier-0 is built, descent-from-seats awaits S8 (registry_hierarchy 'hierarchy descent' row). The invariant is enforceable today on the built path.
required_receipt: Retrieval trace for a probe query shows routing -> document -> tree descent -> parent/child order with no un-routed child fetches.

verbatim: The original query remains a protected retrieval lane.
action: Enforce: the role=='original' recall lane is always present in the query plan and its candidates are never displaced by expansion lanes; assert in planned-pool fusion tests that removing all expansion lanes still yields the original lane's results.
why: The original query is the only lane whose intent is certain; losing it converts expansion errors into total failures.
feasibility_note: TERMINOLOGY (registry_retrieval 'protected lane'): canonical = protected_recall_lane (role=='original'). Three unrelated 'protection' mechanisms share the verb — recall-lane protection (this), reservation seats (reservation_policy.py, P0.3), and per-doc-cap protect_fn. Tests must target the lane sense specifically.
required_receipt: Asserting test: original lane present in every plan; fusion output non-empty and stable when expansion lanes are ablated.

verbatim: Query expansion cannot replace or semantically drift from the original query.
action: Enforce via the existing grounded-expansion gate: expansions pass the grounded threshold, rejected_expansions and lexicon-ID rejections are logged, and the original lane (L53) is never rewritten; S5's alias-registry consumption must feed THROUGH this gate, not around it.
where: grounded vocabulary expansion gate (grounded threshold + rejected_expansions, checklist:597); vocabulary.py; future versioned alias registry (S5)
why: Ungated expansion is the classic drift vector that turns one wrong alias into corpus-wide wrong answers.
feasibility_note: Gate machinery ALREADY exists (registry_alias 'query expansion' row: canonical = grounded vocabulary expansion). The live risk is S5 wiring registry aliases as a bypass; registry explicitly flags this failure mode (b).
required_receipt: Expansion trace shows grounded-threshold pass/reject counts per query; forbidden-vocabulary control rows (three_tier_eval) stay green.

verbatim: Final evidence retains `corpus_id`, `doc_id`, parent/child identity, heading/boundary metadata, and source provenance.
action: Enforce with a payload-contract test: every final evidence row carries corpus_id, doc_id, parent/child identity, heading/boundary metadata, and source provenance; add this as a record-contract column in the 3-tier regression matrix (its 8-field record contract).
where: final evidence assembly (chat_orchestrator evidence packets), Qdrant payloads, regression matrix record contract (checklist:1219-1250)
why: Provenance-less evidence can't be cited, deduped, corpus-bounded, or audited.
feasibility_note: TERMINOLOGY (registry_hierarchy 'boundaries' — worst collision in family): 'boundary' spans child_boundaries (Ghost A provenance), chunker split boundaries, tree section_range, and P0.8 storage boundary. The contract test must name WHICH boundary fields are required per row kind, or it will pass vacuously.
required_receipt: Asserting test iterating final evidence rows across all three tiers: zero rows missing any named field; wired into the S14 matrix.

verbatim: Summary, vocabulary, graph, and generated user-language artifacts carry explicit model/engine, schema version, evidence, and validation status.
action: Enforce at the writer boundary: extend P0.8 schema validators so summary/vocabulary/graph/generated artifacts fail closed without model/engine, schema version, evidence refs, and validation status; the typed-model writer boundary rides the S1 contract touch (plan S1 row).
where: backend/services/storage/schema_validators.py; Ghost A summary contract; corpus_lexicon entries; Neo4j write path
why: Attribution fields are what made the P0.1 empty-model sweep detectable at all; unattributed artifacts are unauditable.
feasibility_note: P0.8 is 3/4 done; the open box is exactly 'typed-model acceptance at Mongo writer boundary' (critique table), and the plan co-schedules it with S1 to touch the contract file once. Summary side already carries summary_model (P0.1 verified 100% attributed).
required_receipt: Writer-boundary test: an artifact missing model/engine or schema_version is REJECTED (non-zero/raised), run green in-container; S1 commit cites it.

verbatim: No empty-model or extractive fallback placeholder is represented as a valid abstractive summary.
action: Keep the P0.1 verify as a standing gate: schedule/require `p0_1_summary_integrity.py verify` (all 4 corpora, exit 0, 0 empty-model) before and after any summary-writing pass; empty-model placeholders may never re-enter via backfill or fallback paths.
who: script (backend/scripts/p0_1_summary_integrity.py) run by junior-agent per summary pass
where: Mongo parent_chunks / Qdrant summary points (summary_model field), all 4 corpora
why: Placeholder summaries poison Funnel-A breadth and every rollup above them.
feasibility_note: Gate is proven: verify PASSED all 4 corpora on 2026-07-13 (critique line 70: 0 empty-model; 86,880+9,453+1,009+203 attributed). The invariant is now regression-prevention. Canonical term: 'empty summary_model placeholder' (registry_leftover).
required_receipt: p0_1_summary_integrity.py verify exit 0 with 0 empty-model counts, attached to every summary-writing run (S4 especially).

verbatim: No query- or document-specific topic rules are hardcoded into retrieval. Curated aliases belong in versioned data, not Python conditionals.
action: Two-part enforcement: (1) standing review rule — no query/document-specific conditionals in retrieval code paths; (2) S5 migrates the THREE existing curated alias stores (CONCEPT_ALIASES query_semantics.py:527, entity_aliases.json, canonical_families.json) into the versioned alias registry rather than adding a fourth store.
where: backend/services/retriever/query_semantics.py:527 (CONCEPT_ALIASES); neo4j_writer.py:43,46 (entity_aliases.json, canonical_families.json); future versioned alias registry
why: Hardcoded topic rules are unversionable, untestable per-corpus, and rot silently.
feasibility_note: TERMINOLOGY COLLISION (registry_alias 'curated aliases'): the phrase names BOTH the retrieval-side CONCEPT_ALIASES (what this invariant means) AND the graph-side canonical_families mechanism (different keyspace: canonicalize_entity_name/NFKD vs normalize_identity). S5 must keep the two keyspaces distinct while versioning both; merging them would corrupt the vector-graph join (cf. entity_id divergence, 'Box2D-Lite' example).
required_receipt: grep of retriever services shows zero topic-term conditionals; CONCEPT_ALIASES literal removed from Python and served from the versioned registry with schema version + provenance; asserting test green.

verbatim: Negative controls must fail closed when the corpus lacks the answer.
action: Enforce via heldout negative_control shape rows: unanswerable-in-corpus questions must produce refusal (REFUSAL_RE match), tested on ALL THREE tiers; keep this distinct from the forbidden-vocabulary control in three_tier_eval.
where: run_heldout_eval negative_control rows; chat answerability gate (services/answerability_tuning.py); regression matrix row 9
why: Failing open on absent knowledge is the worst RAG failure mode — confident fabrication.
feasibility_note: TERMINOLOGY (registry_eval 'negative controls'): two different assertions share the phrase — (i) refusal of unanswerable questions (THIS invariant) and (ii) forbidden-vocabulary expansion control. Also note the answerability gate is intentionally TWO mirror implementations (chat refusal vs retriever sufficiency, registry_retrieval); tune shared knobs in answerability_tuning.py, never one side alone.
required_receipt: Negative-control heldout rows pass (refusal) on qdrant_only, qdrant_mongo, qdrant_mongo_graph in the per-change three-tier run.

verbatim: Pressure gates, provider cooldowns, durable leases, and idempotent replay remain active during all backfills.
action: Pre-flight every backfill run with a config assertion that pressure gates, provider cooldowns, durable leases, and idempotent replay are ACTIVE (not env-defaulted off); close the two open lease-matrix cells (restart-before-expiry, purge-beyond-lease) before the next mass pass (S4).
who: backend service (ingestion worker) + junior-agent (P0.6 lease tests)
where: ingestion worker lease/gate machinery; backend P0.6 test matrix; S4/S11 batch runners
why: The S4 paid pass and S11 burst are exactly where an inactive gate converts a bug into a bill.
feasibility_note: Machinery exists (P0.6 is 11 done / 1 partial); the PARTIAL is the lease test matrix — two untested cells named in the critique table. Aligns with user's silent-fallback-accounting rule (preflight canary before batch spend).
required_receipt: Backfill run log opens with gate/cooldown/lease/replay ACTIVE assertions; P0.6 lease matrix cells restart-before-expiry + purge-beyond-lease each have a green asserting test.

verbatim: API keys and provider credentials are never printed, committed, or stored in plaintext artifacts.
action: Enforce with (1) pre-commit/CI secret scan over the repo and docs/baselines artifacts, (2) run-log review that batch runners print key NAMES not values, (3) continue the disposable-key practice for burst providers.
where: repo-wide; docs/baselines/*.json; batch runner logs; RunPod/provider configs
why: A leaked provider key in a committed baseline artifact is unrevokable history.
feasibility_note: Consistent with existing practice (disposable keys for goal-mode ops per user memory). Baseline artifacts inspected this session contain no credentials.
required_receipt: Secret-scan pass (zero findings) on the checklist-era commits and docs/baselines/; scan wired so a finding fails the run.

verbatim: Embeddings and optional LLMs may generate candidates or enrich fields, but only deterministic evidence-backed policy may grant final document seats.
action: Enforce the seat-granting boundary in review + tests: only deterministic evidence-backed policy (reservation_policy / ranking_policy / shelf_engine rules) grants final document seats; embeddings/LLM outputs may only produce candidates or enrich fields. Concretely: S8 must wire shelf_reserve THROUGH reservation_policy — a second independent seat path is banned.
why: Deterministic seats are what make selection auditable and eval-comparable; LLM-granted seats are unreproducible.
feasibility_note: TERMINOLOGY (registry_leftover 'document seats'): 'seat' spans two granularities — evidence seat (reservation/ranking_policy) vs document seat (librarian shelf_engine). No literal Seat class exists; grep 'seat' in docstrings/diagnostics. The duplicate-seat-system risk is explicitly flagged; S8's design already bans it ('never the old unconditional floor').
required_receipt: Seat diagnostics show every seated doc with a deterministic reason code; asserting test that disabling the LLM changes NO seat grants (only enrichment/explanations).

verbatim: An LLM may explain an already-seated path but may not add, remove, or protect books in the final selection.
action: Enforce explain-not-select as a code-boundary test: the LLM explanation stage (Phase 4 'explain an already-seated path') runs strictly after final selection and its output cannot mutate the seated set; assert selection hash identical before/after the explain call.
where: librarian Phase 4 (LLM amplification) seam; final_selector / seat diagnostics
why: Keeps the trust boundary of L69 airtight at the last stage where an LLM touches results.
feasibility_note: Phase 4 is 0/8 built (critique table), so today this is vacuously true; the invariant matters when Phase-4 work lands (post-S8). The 'explain-not-select' box is one of the 8 Phase-4 items.
required_receipt: Asserting test: seated-set hash pre/post explanation identical; explanation stored as annotation, never as selection input.

verbatim: `polymath_v2`: 84,987 summary points; 67,953 have explicit empty `summary_model`.
action: Re-check the number: it matches docs/baselines/BASELINE_2026-07-13.json (summary_integrity.polymath_v2: 84,987 / 67,953, captured 02:52Z @d3159b8) — but it is SUPERSEDED as current state by the same-day P0.1 green verify (86,880 points, 0 empty-model). Relabel the ledger section 'pre-P0.1 census baseline (frozen)' or restamp with post-P0.1 counts per Standing Rule 3.
why: A 'Current Durable Baseline' that contradicts the current durable state misleads every agent that reads it before dispatch.
feasibility_note: Numbers verified against the census artifact this session (exact match). Staleness verified against PLAN_CRITIQUE line 19/70 (V receipts). This is the exact case Standing Rule 3 was written for.
required_receipt: Ledger edit relabeling the section as frozen pre-P0.1 census (pointer to BASELINE_2026-07-13.json) OR restamped counts from a fresh capture_raptor_baseline.py run, dated.

verbatim: `ecommerce_AI_FILM_SCHOOL`: 9,375 summary points; 5,796 have explicit empty `summary_model`.
